trends.name: Content Fragmentation Acceleration
trends.description: Media consumption fracturing across proliferating platforms and channels, collapsing shared cultural moments and forcing legacy broadcasters to reinvent value extraction.

trends.name: Ad Spend Migration to Digital
trends.description: Advertising dollars accelerating out of legacy channels into digital and social, structurally undermining the revenue base of traditional media incumbents.

signals.name: Streaming Platform Profitability Crisis
signals.description: Streaming services facing simultaneous pressure from content costs, subscriber saturation, and advertiser demand for performance metrics — triggering consolidation.

signals.name: Consumer Fatigue with Subscription Proliferation
signals.description: Household spending on streaming subscriptions hitting a ceiling as consumers rationalize multiple services into fewer bundles.

trends.name: Slowdown of Global Growth Engine
trends.description: Global GDP growth decelerating to 2.8% in 2024-25, with US growth slowing to 1.7% and Eurozone at 1.4%, marking the end of post-pandemic bounce.

trends.name: Geopolitical Fragmentation of Trade
trends.description: Rising geopolitical friction reshaping trade flows, with economic policy divergence between major blocs creating parallel economic zones.

signals.name: Manufacturing Sector in Excess Supply
signals.description: Global manufacturing operating with significant idle capacity as demand from Eurozone and key markets weakens, signaling deeper structural rebalancing.

signals.name: US Labor Market Softening
signals.description: Early indicators of US labor market loosening after historic tightness, raising recession probability and complicating Fed policy trajectory.

trends.name: Private Markets as Core Allocation
trends.description: Institutional and private investors moving illiquid assets like private debt and infrastructure from alternative to core portfolio allocations.

trends.name: Trump Policy Uncertainty Premium
trends.description: US political unpredictability adding a new volatility layer to global markets, with trade and fiscal policy shifts repricing risk across asset classes.

signals.name: Rate Cuts Supporting Risk Assets
signals.description: Fed rate cutting cycle beginning to relieve pressure on interest-rate sensitive assets, creating tailwinds for biotech, Bitcoin, and gold.

signals.name: Re-emergent Inflation Risk in 2025
signals.description: Markets pricing in aggressive rate cuts while underlying conditions — particularly in services and housing — sustain inflationary pressure that could derail the cutting cycle.

trends.name: Experience-Led Travel Motivation
trends.description: Travel decisions increasingly driven by specific experiences, cultural moments, and personal meaning rather than destination prestige or convenience.

signals.name: Concert and Event Tourism Surge
signals.description: Major music tours and sporting events becoming primary travel catalysts, with fans planning international trips specifically around cultural events.

signals.name: Travel Trend Synchronization Across Markets
signals.description: Global travelers showing convergent preferences for authenticity, wellness, and slow travel despite different geographic starting points.

trends.name: Multi-Speed Global Economic Divergence
trends.description: World economy splitting into distinct growth tracks — robust Emerging Asia, moderating US, recovering Europe — ending the assumption of synchronized global cycles.

trends.name: Emerging Asia Regional Connectivity Rise
trends.description: Asian economies deepening intra-regional trade and investment ties, reducing dependence on Western markets and creating independent growth momentum.

signals.name: US Economic Cycle Becoming More Balanced
signals.description: Extraordinary US post-pandemic outperformance moderating toward trend growth, reducing American exceptionalism premium in global portfolios.

signals.name: Hot Debate: AI Investment Returns Timing
signals.description: Significant market uncertainty about when and whether massive AI infrastructure investment will translate into measurable productivity and earnings gains.

scenarios.name: World of Anomalies
scenarios.description: A scenario where positive economic outlook coexists with persistent structural anomalies — high debt, geopolitical fragmentation, tech disruption — requiring investors to navigate without historical precedent.

trends.name: Sustainability Mandates in Food Manufacturing
trends.description: Climate and regulatory pressure forcing food and beverage manufacturers to embed sustainability into operations, supply chains, and product design — beyond voluntary commitment.

signals.name: F&B Manufacturers Struggling to Keep Pace with Customers
signals.description: UK and Ireland food manufacturers reporting significant concern about ability to meet rapidly evolving consumer expectations in 2025, particularly around technology adoption.

signals.name: Cybersecurity Emerging as F&B Operational Risk
signals.description: Food and beverage sector citing cybersecurity threats as a top operational concern, reflecting growing digital dependency of industrial food systems.

trends.name: Five Convergent Innovation Platforms
trends.description: Artificial Intelligence, public blockchains, multiomic sequencing, energy storage, and robotics converging simultaneously to catalyze accelerated transformation across all economic sectors.

trends.name: AI-Enabled Drug Discovery
trends.description: Artificial intelligence compressing pharmaceutical R&D timelines from decades to years, with over 30% of new drugs potentially incorporating AI techniques in discovery and trial design by 2025.

signals.name: In-Silico Drug Trials Emerging
signals.description: AI enabling computer-simulated clinical trial phases, reducing time and cost of pharmaceutical development while improving candidate selection accuracy.

signals.name: Robotics Entering Physical Economy at Scale
signals.description: Autonomous robotic systems beginning to penetrate logistics, manufacturing, and healthcare delivery, transitioning from demonstration to operational deployment.

trends.name: Platform Convergence Driving Exponential Value
trends.description: Five major technology platforms — AI, blockchains, multiomics, energy storage, robotics — beginning to interact and amplify each other, creating non-linear growth trajectories.

trends.name: Multiomic Sequencing Transforming Medicine
trends.description: Tools reading DNA, RNA, and proteins simultaneously enabling personalized medicine, early disease detection, and biological engineering at population scale.

signals.name: Public Blockchain Infrastructure Maturing
signals.description: Layer-2 scaling solutions and institutional adoption making blockchain infrastructure viable for mainstream financial and data applications beyond speculation.

trends.name: AI Democratization of Creative Production
trends.description: AI tools lowering barriers to high-quality content creation, enabling smaller teams and individual creators to produce at scales previously requiring large studios.

trends.name: Creator Economy Mainstreaming
trends.description: Individual content creators becoming primary media entities with global reach, bypassing traditional media infrastructure and brand gatekeepers.

signals.name: AI Already in Most Creators' Workflows
signals.description: Significant majority of creative professionals reporting active AI use in their production processes in 2024, marking transition from experimentation to standard practice.

signals.name: Authenticity Valued Over Polish in Content
signals.description: Audience preference shifting toward raw, genuine content over highly produced material — paradoxically as production quality becomes easier to fake with AI.

trends.name: Art Market Contraction After Pandemic Boom
trends.description: Global art market correcting from pandemic-era highs as economic headwinds, rising interest rates, and buyer hesitation reduce transaction volumes and prices.

signals.name: Auction Houses Becoming Experiential Entertainment
signals.description: Major auction houses increasingly staging events as theater — spectacle, narrative, celebrity — to maintain relevance and drive premium prices in a contracting market.

signals.name: Hong Kong Art Market Under Pressure
signals.description: Hong Kong's role as Asian art hub facing structural challenge from geopolitical friction and shifting collector behavior toward Singapore and other markets.
